specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Can be fulfilled also by completing an industrial internship.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt, English-language Bachelor's degree accepted, English B2 or English as a first language at German Abitur level accepted.

course_content_summary: Master's programme in Advanced Quantum Physics including experimental and theoretical courses. Fields covered: quantum optics, quantum matter, laser cooling, quantum gases, laser physics, superconductors, Josephson junctions, open and many-body quantum systems, mathematical quantum theory, quantum information theory. Practical contents in quantum labs: lasers, elements of quantum optics, superconductors, photons, photon statistics. Modules from a neighbouring field or industrial internship required.

program_name: Advisory and Innovation Services in Agri-Food Systems
course_content_summary: Advisory Services, Innovation Services, Agri-Food Systems, Sustainable Innovations, Agricultural Sciences, agricultural production, economics, engineering, social science skills, consulting, promoting innovation, farm management, technical specialisation, problem-solving, innovation processes, economic factors, decision-making, value chains, agri-food industry, agricultural knowledge and innovation systems (AKIS), planning, implementing, evaluating policies, governance, coordination, innovation networks, knowledge systems, knowledge transfer.

Research-based and methods-oriented program in Agricultural Economics (AgEcon) focusing on economic, agricultural, and development policy relationships within the agricultural and food economy, environment, and rural areas. Central topics include economic growth, sustainability, and social justice in international and development-related contexts. The curriculum provides comprehensive education in economic and agricultural theories and methodologies, with application to specific problems in the international agricultural and food sector. Key competencies developed are theoretical concepts of agricultural economics, quantitative and qualitative research methods, integration of sustainability issues into policy analysis, interdisciplinary analysis of complex systems, and communication and presentation techniques.

program_name: Agricultural Sciences in the Tropics and Subtropics (AgriTropics)
course_content_summary: Agricultural Sciences in Tropics and Subtropics (AgriTropics) is an international Master's program focusing on development-oriented agricultural research. It addresses challenges like population growth, climate change, and resource depletion, aiming for agroecological, sustainable, and highly productive agriculture. The curriculum provides broad knowledge in tropical agriculture, combining interdisciplinary approaches to train students in development-oriented agricultural research and prepare them to lead multidisciplinary teams. Key skills include specific knowledge in development-oriented agricultural research, competence in multidisciplinary and interdisciplinary cooperation, assessment of natural resource influence on agricultural production in tropical and subtropical agroecosystems, evaluation of material cycles (water, nitrogen, carbon), reduction of post-harvest losses, promotion of innovation in product processing, management of gender-sensitive development and extension approaches for sustainable agricultural production, improving marketability of agricultural products, increasing rural incomes, and applying integrated knowledge for management recommendations and sustainability strategies in the context of climate change. Graduates find opportunities in international/national agricultural research centers, ministries for development cooperation, land/environmental protection, consulting firms, NGOs, universities, research institutions, agricultural businesses, and EU institutions. Internships are recommended, and the program qualifies for doctoral studies.

program_name: Air Quality Control, Solid Waste and Waste Water Process Engineering (MSc WASTE)

german_requirements.notes: International students without prior knowledge of German must attend the intensive German language course in September (free of charge).

course_content_summary: The Master of Science in Air Quality Control, Solid Waste and Waste Water Process Engineering (MSc WASTE) is a four-semester, international English-taught program. It focuses on fundamentals of environmental and process engineering through compulsory advanced modules. Students specialize in Air Quality Control, Solid Waste, or Waste Water Process Engineering, each including a core module, elective modules, and practical work or an industrial internship. The program allows for individual study plans, broadening or specializing studies, excursions, and practical work in research facilities. The fourth semester is dedicated to the Master's thesis, which can be done at the university, a company, or another research facility. German language courses are provided for students without prior proficiency, with alternative interdisciplinary modules for those exempt. A double degree program with Universidade Federal do Paraná (UFPR) in Brazil is also possible.

The two-year Master's program in American Studies offers comprehensive training in methods and theories of American Studies, covering literatures, cultures, history, and society of the United States. Courses range from colonial past to contemporary themes in race, class, gender, religious, ethnic, and regional studies. The program emphasizes student flexibility, interdisciplinary curriculum, autonomy, academic collaboration, and exchange. Key areas include American Literature and Culture, Interdisciplinary Profile, Research, and Practice. An introductory module builds common knowledge and research/writing skills. The core curriculum focuses on fundamental theoretical frameworks. Students attend courses in other departments to create unique academic profiles, discuss MA thesis projects in a colloquium, and gain professional experience through applied American Studies, organizing exhibitions, festivals, workshops, or tutoring. Internships are encouraged. Career opportunities include academia, journalism, publishing, public relations, consulting, international organizations, new media, and public administration.

english_requirements.notes: For more information and other language tests that can be recognised, please refer to the application information website: www.uni-hohenheim.de/en/application-animalscience-info.

course_content_summary: Animal Science, resource efficiency, animal welfare, sustainable breeding, husbandry, nutrition, health of farm animals, high-quality animal foodstuffs, current research topics, species-compliant livestock husbandry, dynamic research environment, self-selected specialisation, nutrition and feed, genomics and breeding, health and behaviour, microbiota of the digestive tract, animal and environment, sustainability, ethical issues, social, economic, environmental consequences of agricultural science, animal nutrition, animal hygiene, animal behaviour, animal breeding, animal husbandry, anatomy, physiology, endocrinology, genetics, microbiology, feed science, animal husbandry techniques, modern research methods, scientific issues, farm animals, hypotheses, research, development, management, marketing, biotechnology companies, agricultural operations, food and feed industry, ministries, environmental and agricultural agencies, expert journalism, public relations, university and non-university research institutions, business consulting, sales, breeding and stable construction companies, doctoral programme.

Applied & Environmental Geoscience (AEG) is an international, research-oriented, interdisciplinary Master's program focusing on the evaluation and solution of environmental problems, particularly in the subsurface. Key areas include pollution of drinking water supplies, non-sustainable use of natural resources, and the impact of climate and land-use change on soil and water quality. The curriculum integrates classical natural sciences (mathematics, physics, chemistry, biology) with modern technology to understand physical, chemical, and biological mechanisms. Core fields covered are hydrogeology, environmental modelling, environmental chemistry, environmental microbiology, and environmental physics. Specializations are offered in Hydrogeology, Environmental Chemistry and Environmental Microbiology, and Environmental Physics. The program emphasizes multidisciplinary approaches and independent problem-solving.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Mathematics, Computer Science, and Artificial Intelligence
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: minimum of 10 ECTS credits in each of these three subject areas

accepted_tests.test_name: ICC Certificate in English for Business Purposes

english_requirements.notes: English is the applicant’s native language exempts. Applicants holding a degree from an English-language study programme are exempt. The certificate must not be older than three years at the time of application.

course_content_summary: Applied Artificial Intelligence (MAAI) Master's programme, machine learning, data analysis, artificial neural networks, practical application of AI in healthcare, engineering, logistics, business informatics, software engineering, application development, AI research.

english_requirements.notes: We may also personally interview applicants in order to test their English skills. If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: Curriculum includes International Project Management, Software Architecture, IT Security, Advanced Database, Software Development Practice, Advanced Computer Science. Students are guided into standard areas of full stack development (front end, middleware, back end / databases and infrastructure) and individual specialisations (Business Computing, Mobile Computing and Usability Engineering and UX Design). Graduates work in software and IT industry for companies like ABB, Airbus, Accenture, Audi, Bosch, BMW, BASF, Deutsche Boerse, DKFZ, Triumph, PWC, SAP, Verivox, and SMEs. Qualifies for public sector, research, and doctoral studies.

program_name: Applied Data Science and Artificial Intelligence (MSc)

english_requirements.notes: We may also personally interview applicants in order to test their English skills. If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: Applied Data Science, Artificial Intelligence, business application data scientist, technical courses, statistical courses, big data architectures, data mining algorithms, text mining algorithms, neural networks, predictive analytics, creative questioning techniques, visualisation, data storytelling, ethics, international law, practical experience, case studies, Master's thesis.

program_name: Architecture – Design for the Built Environment (MA)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Undergraduate degree (Bachelor's or 'Diplom') in Architecture with at least 210 credit points. If Bachelor's degree comprises 180 credit points, an additional 30 credit points must be acquired by taking a preliminary course. This course entails practical experience in an architectural office in Germany as well as a design project. The practical pre-course must be started and successfully completed no later than the winter semester to ensure entry into the regular programme for the following summer semester.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the required test may be waived. Personal interviews may be conducted to test English skills.

course_content_summary: Reflexive approach towards architecture and the city, developing architectural ideas, concepts, theories, and implementing them in architectural design within a specific urban fabric. Project-based teaching methods, field trips, research projects, academic events (summer schools, conferences).

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Astrophysics and Particle Physics relevant knowledge
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Proof of knowledge required in particular areas.

english_requirements.notes: Native speaker exempt, English-language Bachelor's degree accepted, English as first language at German Abitur level accepted.

course_content_summary: Master of Science in Astro and Particle Physics, international, research-oriented, two-year program by Kepler Center, Physics Department, Faculty of Science, University of Tübingen. Involves Institute for Astronomy and Astrophysics, Physical Institute, and Institute for Theoretical Physics. Research focus: Astronomy, Astrophysics, Astroparticle Physics, Particle Physics. Connects particle physics, astrophysics, cosmology, experimental physics, theoretical physics. Investigates origin, structure, evolution of universe, properties of elementary particles under extreme conditions. Coordinated PhD program: Particles, Fields and Messengers of the Universe.

Laboratory skills, organisational skills, practical proficiency in research, personal skills, broad degree, flexible program, international, career-oriented, English instruction, writing courses, presenting courses, international student body, worldwide peer network, lab rotations abroad, fundamentals of life and disease, nucleic acids, proteins, lipids, sugars, cell formation, organism formation, disease mechanisms, drug development, combines biology and chemistry, topics include immunology, cell biology, molecular biology, structural biology, biophysics, virology, microbiology, pathobiochemistry, toxicology, pharmacology, bioinformatics, developmental biology, neuroscience, molecular medicine.

The Master's program in Bioeconomy focuses on sectors utilizing biological resources and biotechnological processes for bio-based goods and services. Students examine the entire value chain of bio-based products, including raw material production in agricultural ecosystems, properties and supply logistics of biological resources, biotechnological and industrial conversion processes, and marketing and consumption. The program addresses environmental, social, and economic dimensions of the bioeconomy from micro- and macro-level perspectives, considering innovation, institutions, and policies. It is an interdisciplinary program offered by the faculties of Agricultural Sciences, Natural Sciences, and Business, Economics and Social Sciences, enabling systematic analysis of bio-based value chains. Graduates gain expertise to understand bio-based value chains from the perspectives of companies focusing on new resources/processes/products, established producers introducing renewable resources/biotechnological processes, actors gauging need/acceptance, and organizations supporting development through research/advisory services.

german_requirements.notes: Required for Variant B and C students.

course_content_summary: Bioinformatics, computer science, life sciences, biological problems, high throughput technologies, bioinformatics methods, interdisciplinary team, algorithmic thinking, efficient analysis, advanced methods, visualisation, mass data storage, research, hands-on approach, genomics, transcriptomics, protein design, drug design, microbiome analysis, systems biology.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from countries whose native language is English do not have to prove their English language skills, e.g., USA, Canada, UK, etc.

course_content_summary: Master's in Biological Sciences with flexible curriculum. Priority tracks include CELLULAR & MOLECULAR BIOLOGY (biochemistry, genetics, biophysics, macromolecules, Chemical Biology), DISEASE BIOLOGY (biomedical research, immunology, infections, cell death, toxicology, stem cell research, medical microbiology, cancer research, neurodegenerative diseases, biogerontology, molecular and systems biology, cell signalling, proteostasis), and ECOLOGY, EVOLUTION & BEHAVIOUR (animal behaviour, ecology, evolution, physiology, neurobiology, organisms, communities, environment, interactions). Focus on research questions and methodological approaches. Includes a mandatory two-month internship at an external research facility or private sector company. All courses taught in English. Prepares students for PhD studies.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants can be exempted from producing proof of language proficiency if they declare English to be their mother tongue and are citizens of one of the following countries: the United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the United States of America, Antigua and Barbuda, the Bahamas, Barbados, Dominica, Grenada, Jamaica, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, Belize, or Guyana OR have completed a Bachelor's or Master's programme with English as the language of instruction at a university in a country listed above or in a member state of the European Union.

course_content_summary: Interdisciplinary and research-oriented Master's programme in Biotechnology. Focuses on properties, production processes, and applications of enzymes and microorganisms in the food industry, supplying industries, and for bioanalytical purposes. Explores enzymatic production processes, biochemical methods including purification, characterisation, immobilisation of enzymes, enzyme kinetics, gene expression, and mutagenesis of recombinant enzymes. Covers pathogens and hygiene in food production, utilizing microorganisms and enzymes in the life science industry. Core themes include innovation and safe processing.

Functional neuroanatomy, sensory systems, motor systems, neurophysiology, cellular biology of neurons, molecular biology of neurons, cellular biology of glia, molecular biology of glia, neurochemistry, neurotransmitters, neurogenetics, developmental neurobiology, genetic basis of neurological diseases, molecular basis of neurological diseases, genetic basis of psychiatric diseases, molecular basis of psychiatric diseases, neuroglia, microscopy techniques, molecular imaging techniques, tissue culture, tissue engineering, stem cells, clinical neuroscience, neural diseases, genetics, biochemistry, molecular biology, cell biology.

english_requirements.notes: Graduates of English-language Bachelor's study programmes require a certificate from the university from which they obtained their Bachelor's degree (minimum six semesters of study).

course_content_summary: Research-oriented Master's program for international graduates in chemistry or related fields. Offers specialization across broad research areas, advanced hands-on laboratory training, deepening conceptual understanding and analytical skills, and preparation for independent research projects. Flexible structure with specializations in Sustainable Synthesis and Catalysis, Smart Materials and Functional Molecules, Biological Chemistry and Biotechnology, and Theory and Simulation in Chemistry and Materials Sciences.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from Australia, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Canada, Malta, New Zealand, Singapore, South Africa or the United States do not have to prove their English language skills.

course_content_summary: The Master's programme in Chemistry focuses on advanced, research-related courses in inorganic, organic, and physical chemistry, as well as subjects related to life sciences and material sciences. It includes compulsory elective subjects and 'focus area courses' with practical experience and research projects under intensive supervision. These courses provide a foundation for the Master’s thesis and subsequent doctoral studies.

required_degree_domains: biology with a specialisation in neurosciences

english_requirements.notes: Higher education degree in a study programme with English as the sole language of instruction accepted. CEFR C1 level or higher shown on the higher education entrance qualification accepted.

course_content_summary: The Master's program in Cognitive Systems at Ulm University focuses on understanding cognition and engineering intelligence in humans and machines. It covers perception, learning, memory, reasoning, and interaction, integrating theoretical, empirical, and computational approaches. Students learn to model and implement cognitive abilities, investigate functions through experiments and simulations, and apply knowledge to develop intelligent systems. Key areas include intelligent robotics, cognitive assistance systems, smart vehicles and devices, AI in healthcare, and human-computer interaction. The program emphasizes interdisciplinary study, connecting human cognition with machine intelligence through psychophysical studies, psychological testing, and the development of intelligent technical systems.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Basic knowledge in closely related fields
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Basic knowledge in six or more of the following eight topics, to the extent of at least one module each.

english_requirements.notes: Higher education degree in a study programme with English as the sole language of instruction; CEFR C1 level or higher shown on the higher education entrance qualification; A level of language proficiency that is partly at CEFR C1 or lower will not be recognised.

course_content_summary: Communication and Information Technology degree program offers specialisations in four tracks: Communications Circuits and Systems, Sensor Circuits and Systems, Communications Engineering, and Intelligent Systems. Strong foundation in integrated mixed-signal and RF circuits, RF engineering, radar design, wireless communication, signal processing, optical communication devices, and machine learning. Communications Engineering focuses on modern digital communication methods, coding, networking, advanced modulation techniques, mobile communication methods and systems. Communications Circuits and Systems emphasizes hardware aspects, microwave/millimetre-wave engineering, microelectronics, optoelectronic devices, millimetre waves, microfabrication, theoretical instruction, hands-on laboratory experience. Sensor Circuits and Systems focuses on integrated circuit and sensor design, mixed-signal and RF integrated circuits, mm-wave engineering, integrated sensors, sensor interfaces. Intelligent Systems track for software-oriented students with information system technology or computer engineering background, focusing on machine learning, pattern recognition, and secure system engineering.

program_name: Comparative and Middle East Politics and Society (CMEPS)

english_requirements.notes: Students are exempt from certifying their English language skills under certain conditions (mother tongue, previous study experience in English-language programmes); please consult our homepage for details. IELTS writing section at least 7.0 required.

german_requirements.notes: German language skills are not a prerequisite at either of the partner institutions.

course_content_summary: The CMEPS program focuses on Comparative and Middle East Politics and Society. It includes an intensive introductory political science course, a study trip, and profiles in 'Political Science' (research methods, political science theories, advanced Arabic) and 'Arabic' (intensive Arabic language courses). Core courses cover Comparative Politics of the Middle East, Regime Change and Democratisation, Comparative Theories of Political Science, and International Cooperation and Development. Elective courses include international relations, political economy of the Middle East, international migration governance, social movements, development cooperation, conflict analysis, political impact of new media, and political regime analysis. Students complete a semester abroad at a partner university (American University in Cairo), taking Arabic, political science, and elective courses, as well as German classes. The program culminates in a Master's thesis, thesis proposals, an International CMEPS Colloquium, oral defense, and a mandatory internship.

english_requirements.notes: MoI is not accepted.

german_requirements.notes: you don't need a proof for German language proficiency (since the programme is taught in English)

course_content_summary: Dialogue systems, machine translation, search engines, Computational Linguistics (CL), automatic processing of written and spoken language, core computational linguistics, applied natural language processing, speech processing, CL team laboratory, CL research module, Master's theses, elective modules, Computer Science, Linguistics.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a German Abitur including English at least from the year eight to the last year of school with the final grade of "good" and applicants whose native language is English or who graduated from a school at which English was the language of instruction in a country with English as the first official language do not need to submit additional proof of English competence.

course_content_summary: Computational linguistics research, practical skills, grammar formalisms, machine learning, information retrieval, dialectometry, computer-aided language learning, project-driven work, academic basis, problem-solving.

program_name: Computational Mechanics of Materials and Structures (COMMAS)
course_content_summary: Computational Mechanics of Materials and Structures (COMMAS) MSc program focuses on the development and implementation of numerical methods and simulation techniques for complex engineering problems. Students learn practical approaches to model, understand, predict, and validate responses of materials and structures. It is an interdisciplinary program with cooperation between Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering, and Aerospace Engineering faculties. Strong collaborations with research centers and industrial partners. Graduates can apply knowledge to simulating automobile crash tests, predicting material failure loads, modelling biological tissues, developing functional materials for robotics. Research-oriented, preparing graduates for careers in universities, laboratories, and research centers.

Neural data analysis, models of neural coding and computation, physical and physiological bases of neural recordings, brain imaging, computational motor control, computational vision, rehabilitation robotics, brain computer interfaces, systems neuroscience, neurophysiology, basic mechanisms of learning and memory, mathematics, statistics, programming, machine learning for neuroscience, theoretical neuroscience, behaviour, cognition, essay rotation, laboratory rotation, Master's thesis.

Master's degree in Computer and Information Science, specializing in data science, visualisation (visual computing), human-computer interaction (HCI). Focus on exploratory analysis and visualisation of large information spaces. Strong research orientation and research-driven teaching. Specialisations include Data Science, Interactive Systems, Software and Systems, Visual Computing, Algorithmics. Mentoring programme provided.

english_requirements.notes: MoI is not accepted.

german_requirements.notes: Programme is taught in English; no proof for German language proficiency needed.

course_content_summary: Computer Science, Software Engineering, Autonomous Systems in Computer Science (AUT), Service Technology and Engineering (STE), Visual Computing (VC), robotics, visual computing, computer engineering, service technology and engineering, database systems, parallel systems, distributed systems.

program_name: Computer Science for Industry and Business (MSc COSIB)

english_requirements.notes: Waivers: Evidence of five years of continuous English classes (German secondary school up to Abitur, min. grade 'befriedigend'); Successful completion of min. four subject-related courses held in English in a previous relevant degree programme or exchange programme (EEA/Switzerland); Successful completion of min. three subject-related courses in English and the writing of the final thesis in English in a previous relevant degree programme (EEA/Switzerland); High school or Bachelor's degree or study exchange certificate (min. one semester) from an English educational institution from USA, UK, Canada, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, South Africa.

course_content_summary: Compact further qualification in computer science, career-oriented practical component, algorithms, data structures, programming languages, efficient algorithms, Data Science, data analysis, processing, visualisation of data, big data, machine learning, statistical methods, data visualisation, IT labour market, compulsory internship, Master's thesis in a company, career services.

english_requirements.notes: Degree in English accepted

course_content_summary: Master’s programme for corporate communication management, comprehensive focus on communications, public relations, CSR communication, internal communication, change management, digital marketing, social media. Covers communication of and in organisations, corporate communication and its three pillars. In-depth understanding of corporate communication, comprehensive approach to public relations, internal communication, marketing communication. Applied skills in communication methods, techniques, leadership training. Research-based view on current developments and trends, teaching research project. Core and elective subjects from contemporary management, strategic management, leadership, business management. High employability, network of companies, case studies, company projects, empirical studies. Career opportunities in managerial positions in corporate communications, NPOs, public sector, consultancies, expert positions in internal communication, PR, CSR.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, we may waive the required test.

course_content_summary: Dance Movement Therapy, evidence-based, dance, movement, therapy, physical, emotional, cognitive, social, spiritual integration, arts therapy, creative methods, expression, social connection, emotional well-being, research, clinical practice, psychiatry, psychosomatic medicine, special education, neurology, oncology, cardiovascular medicine, preventive care, psychosomatics, psychotherapy, geriatrics, forensic contexts, paediatrics, intensive medicine, pain, sub-acute care, psychosocial institutions, children, families, special needs, disabilities, early intervention, prevention, education, schools, youth offers, kindergartens.

program_name: Development, Environment, Societies, and History of South Asia

english_requirements.notes: If your first language is English and you are a citizen of a majority English-speaking country OR you earned your Bachelor's degree in a majority English-speaking country, then you do not need to demonstrate English proficiency (Antigua and Barbuda, Australia, the Bahamas, Barbados, Belize, Canada, Dominica, Grenada, Guyana, Ireland, Jamaica, Malta, New Zealand, St Kitts and Nevis, St Lucia, St Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, UK, USA).

german_requirements.notes: Successful completion of the study programme doesn't necessitate German language skills. However, a passive knowledge of German will significantly enhance your access to the university's offerings, student services, and the richness of life in Germany.

course_content_summary: Two-year Master's program at South Asia Institute, Heidelberg University. Taught in English, small groups, interdisciplinary discussions. Research-oriented curriculum covering historical, social, political, economic developments, geography, and environment of South Asia (Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, Sri Lanka). Focus on interdisciplinary paradigms in humanities and social sciences, fostering independent work and self-reflexivity. Students choose a study focus from Political Science, History, Anthropology, Art History and Visual Culture, Development Economics, Geography. Customization with additional courses, including language acquisition. Guest professorships from Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, Sri Lanka. Open talks by professors, writers, social and cultural activists. Offers instruction in ten South Asian languages: Bengali, Dari, Hindi, Classical Tibetan, Nepali, Pali, Sanskrit, Sinhala, Tamil, Urdu, as part of in-house minor programmes.

english_requirements.notes: Degree in English accepted

course_content_summary: Digital Business Management, digital transformation projects, Digital Management, Digital Business Models and Products, Project and Process Management, Algorithms and Data Analytics, process analytics, process design, data analytics, responsible management methods, project management methods, techniques, tools, leadership training, strategic management, leadership, contemporary management, research-based view, current developments, trends, employability, network of companies.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers from UK, Ireland, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, USA, Antigua and Barbuda, Bahamas, Barbados, Dominica, Grenada, Jamaica, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, Belize, Guyana are exempt. Bachelor's studies with English as language of instruction accepted. Bilingual school with English as one language of instruction accepted. English as a school subject during Oberstufe at a German Gymnasium with a minimum grade point average of 'befriedigend' (eight grade points) accepted.

course_content_summary: Interdisciplinary and research-oriented Master's program focusing on Earth and Climate System Science. Covers natural sciences, agricultural sciences, and economic sciences. Key processes in Earth system, human activities, assessment of Earth system status, analysis of indicators. Simulation of Earth system components (regional climate) over land surface, with focus on land use, land management, and agricultural activities. Essential knowledge for end users and decision-makers in climate change. Develops concepts of sustainability, environmental protection, food security, steering Earth's development, and guidance for public and decision-makers. Unique program in Germany, coordinated with international weather, climate, and Earth system research.

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt

course_content_summary: Two-year Master's programme in Economics, English-taught, focusing on modern scientific methods of economics and their application to economic problems. Specialization areas include Econometrics, Applied Economics, International Financial Economics, Macroeconomics, International Economics, Microeconomics, Decision-Making, and Public Economics. Requires a Bachelor's degree with sufficient quantitative background.

program_name: Economics (MSc) with Double Degree Option with the University of Nottingham

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur", including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English; Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies; University degree obtained in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand. The University of Nottingham requires an IELTS of 6.5 (no less than 6.0 in any element) or an equivalent qualification.

course_content_summary: The MSc Economics program offers state-of-the-art education in economics, preparing students for careers in academia, public, and private sectors. Focus areas include Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Quantitative Methods (Statistics, Mathematics, Econometrics). Research excellence covers topics like ICT Adoption on Firm Organisation, Heterogenous Agent Macroeconomics, and International Taxation. Teaching emphasizes contemporary policy issues such as Economics of Taxation, Fiscal Policy during Covid Pandemic, Computational Macroeconomics, and Empirical Topics in International Trade. The university is known for its machine learning cluster.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur", including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English; Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies; University degree obtained in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand

german_requirements.notes: Knowledge of German is not required to complete the programme. However, German classes are available.

course_content_summary: Economics, Finance, Econometrics, Financial Sector, Risk Management, Quantitative Methods, Microeconomics, Macroeconomics, Financial Instruments, Asset Pricing, Options, Futures, Econometric Software (Matlab, GAUSS, SAS, Stata), Data Analysis, Economic Theory, Programming Skills, Financial Stability, Regulatory Offices, Central Banks, Private and Public Financial Institutions, Academia.

The Electrical Engineering Master's program at the University of Stuttgart is a two-year, English-taught program focusing on traditional foundations of electrical engineering and information technology. It does not require German language proficiency. Students choose one of seven specializations: Smart Information Processing, Communication Systems, Nano- and Optoelectronics, Power-Electronic Systems and Technologies, Smart Systems, Electromagnetics and Applications, or Electrical Energy Systems. The program benefits from generous research funding and close industry connections, providing an outstanding education and facilitating a smooth transition to professional careers.

Engineering Cybernetics, Control, Robotics, System dynamics, Modelling and simulation, foundational courses, applied mathematical tools, abstracting complex problems, traditional and modern engineering applications (aerospace, mechanical, biomedical, energy systems), control analysis and design problems, systems theory, dynamical systems modelling and simulation, computational methods.

program_name: Environmental Protection and Agricultural Food Production (EnviroFood)
course_content_summary: Experts for sustainable environmental protection, intensifying food production with environmentally friendly and sustainable production systems. International Master's programme focusing on sustainability and environmental protection, considering ecological, economic, political, and administrative factors. Objectives include analyzing economic, political, and administrative interactions within agroecosystems, understanding physical, biological, and chemical properties of environmental chemicals, model development for environmental systems, conducting simulations, analyzing geodata using geographic information systems, and developing integrative, global problem-solving approaches. Graduates are qualified for environmental and quality management, agricultural and environmental consulting, research and development in international companies and organizations, and research institutions, also qualifying for doctoral projects.

program_name: Environmental Science – Soil, Water and Biodiversity (EnvEuro)

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Proven knowledge in at least two of these areas

course_content_summary: European double Master’s program focusing on sustainable use and management of natural resources in Europe. Specializations include Water Resources, Soil Resources and Land Use, Ecosystems and Biodiversity, Environmental Management, and Climate Change. Emphasizes international collaboration, environmental management of ecosystems, problem identification, and solution development in an international context. Prepares for careers in environmental and quality management, environmental technology, consultancy (agriculture, landscape planning, environmental protection), impact assessment, risk analysis, research and development, and government agencies. Qualifies for doctorate programs.

program_name: Global Management and Digital Competencies (Master of Science)
course_content_summary: Master of Science in Global Management and Digital Competencies. Program completed in two countries, taught entirely in English. Consists of two study blocks of lectures and coursework, and one thesis study block which may involve an internship. First study block at partner universities in France, Ireland, Italy, or Canada; second in Reutlingen. Thesis block location is flexible, often with a company internship globally. Small class sizes, intensive interaction, intercultural and interdisciplinary team work. High academic standard and practical orientation from experienced professors and corporate representatives. Focus on global business and management in the era of digital disruption. Prepares for careers in global top management. AACSB International accredited.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English accepted. Officially recognised higher education degree (e.g. Bachelor's degree) with English as the language of instruction accepted (proof required). The University of Nottingham requires an IELTS of 6.5 (no less than 6.0 in any element) or an equivalent qualification. Language requirements might be subject to change in the future. Even though classes in English are available at all partner universities, it is essential for the double degree experience to have some command over the national language spoken at the partner university.

german_requirements.notes: German is not required. However, German classes are available. Italian is not a formal requirement, as classes in Pavia are taught in English. But it is strongly advised to take at least one language course in preparation for the stay abroad if the student is not yet fluent in Italian. French is also not a formal requirement, as classes in Marseille are taught in English. But it is strongly advised to take at least one language course in preparation for the stay abroad if the student is not yet fluent in French. Students have the possibility to take various language courses at our Language Learning Centre.

course_content_summary: European Economics, double degree, graduate level economics education, integrated year of study, international focus, European economic world, international companies, public sector, Business Administration, Economics, basic principles, theories, concepts, Microeconomics, Trade Theory and Policy, Finance, Quantitative Methods, Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics. Study locations include Tübingen, Pavia (Italy), Nottingham (England), Aix-Marseille (France).

english_requirements.notes: Applicants can be exempted from producing proof of language proficiency if they declare English to be their mother tongue and are citizens of one of the following countries: the United Kingdom, Ireland, Australia, Canada, New Zealand, the United States of America, Antigua and Barbuda, the Bahamas, Barbados, Dominica, Grenada, Jamaica, Saint Kitts and Nevis, Saint Vincent and the Grenadines, Trinidad and Tobago, Belize, Guyana OR have completed a Bachelor's or Master's programme with English as the language of instruction at a university in a country listed above or in a member state of the European Union.

course_content_summary: Modern food processing, transformation of plant-based and animal-based raw materials, value-added, safe and stable food or nutrient formulas. Research-oriented Master's programme focusing on the interface between complex food matrices and technical processes in food development and production. Expertise in equipment and processes for food processing, biogenesis of raw materials, microbiological ecology. Develop food formulas and processing techniques, product-specific processing conditions, automated production processes, in-line sensor technology. Core: development of new technologies for food products meeting consumer expectations and individual nutritional requirements, transfer of basic research concepts into new technological approaches.

New digital technologies, gathering, linking, evaluating information, intelligent products, design and implementation of linked, end-to-end IT systems, sensor to IT system to business model, cyber-physical business system, socio-technical problems, integration of expertise from sensor technology, information systems, business models, innovation-driven, research-qualified information engineer, doctorate qualification.

english_requirements.notes: If applicants have studied completely in English in their previous studies, the required test may be waived.

course_content_summary: Master's in Information Technology focusing on wireless communications, cellular TV phone technology, satellite technology (CubeSat standard), robotics (navigation), and network technology. Students learn to convert, store, protect, process, transmit, and securely retrieve information. Emphasis on implementation, support, and management of computer-based information systems, including software applications and computer hardware. Practice-oriented projects in small working groups.

computer science, electronics, information engineering, information, communication, embedded systems, computer engineering, fundamental methods, scientific skills, development, research, Master's thesis

The MSc Integrated Urbanism and Sustainable Design (IUSD) program at the University of Stuttgart trains urban practitioners to address environmental, cultural, socio-economic, and governance challenges from dynamic urban transformation. It is open to graduates and young professionals in architecture, urban planning, landscape architecture, regional planning, civil engineering, geography, sociology, environmental management, political science, economics, cultural studies, Arabic, African, or Islamic studies, with relevant professional experience. The program emphasizes transnational learning exchange, interdisciplinary teamwork on site-specific projects, and cooperation with local communities and stakeholders. Students acquire reflective and practical skills for integrated, transdisciplinary solutions in planning, managing, and designing landscapes, cities, and buildings. It is a double degree program with Ain Shams University, Cairo.

required_degree_domains: Similar engineering or natural science degrees

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers and students who passed the entirety of their undergraduate studies in Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, the US or the UK are exempt from this rule.

course_content_summary: The Integrative Technologies & Architectural Design Research (ITECH) MSc program is a multidisciplinary, research-oriented, experiment-based program focusing on contemporary aspects of the built environment. It prepares students from design, engineering, construction, and natural sciences for advancements in technological and computational processes. The curriculum merges design, engineering, construction, and natural sciences, emphasizing computational design simulation, fabrication processes, structural and climate engineering, robotics, digital manufacturing, material science, and biology. The program challenges current architectural and engineering practices, re-examining design techniques, methods, and theories. It investigates integrative technological advancements as novel potentials in architecture and construction, preparing students for complex conditions in the building industry, including environmental and economic challenges and emerging technical opportunities. Students engage in cutting-edge computational architectural design techniques, structural and climate engineering, and advanced fabrication and construction technologies, exploring their interrelation as both technical and intellectual ventures.

english_requirements.notes: Continuous stay of at least one year in an English-speaking foreign country as evidenced by studies at an English-speaking university, employment certificates or similar documents. Not applicable to native English speakers (Australia, Canada, Ireland, New Zealand, UK, or USA).

course_content_summary: Master's degree in Intelligent Mechatronic Systems specializing in development and application of mechatronics systems using intelligent methods like AI. Focus on the interface between mechanics, electronics, and computer science to create innovative solutions. Enhances components with artificial intelligence, machine learning, and intelligent control systems. Offers elective courses. Aims to develop autonomous, adaptive, and efficient systems for industries like automotive and robotics. Prepares graduates for challenging jobs in research, development, and industry.

english_requirements.notes: Students must ensure that they are able to follow a lecture on Master's level in English.

course_content_summary: The program focuses on understanding, designing, and implementing complex, distributed, and large intelligent systems. Key areas include artificial intelligence, data science, analysis and structuring of digital information, strategic use of data, and interconnections. It prepares students for scientific and managerial roles in application-oriented computer science, covering both industry and research applications.

english_requirements.notes: An English-language school-leaving certificate or an equivalent English-language university entrance qualification or a professionally qualifying degree from a university programme with a duration of at least three years with English-language coursework and examinations amounting to at least 180 ECTS credits or the equivalent

course_content_summary: Induction period, foundation stage, internal and external business environments, internal company processes, human resource management, leadership, global marketing, international finance and economics, intercultural communication, advanced studies in intercultural management, business environment, cross-disciplinary approach, Master's thesis, empirical research, international companies, organisations, sustainability, corporate responsibility, quantitative and qualitative research, internship, study abroad.

university_name: HTWG Konstanz - University of Applied Sciences
required_degree_domains: Interdisciplinary degree related to Asia and/or business

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Basic knowledge of business and economics required. If Bachelor's degree is less than 210 ECTS, missing points (particularly business prerequisites) can be made up by taking courses at HTWG.

course_content_summary: International Management Asia-Europe (MIM) is a three-semester, full-time Master of Arts program focusing on executive management skills at the crossroads of Europe and Asia. It emphasizes complexity and risk management in international business, offered by the Asian Studies Department at HTWG Konstanz with a favorable student-instructor ratio and small group sizes.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Missing credits in geophysics can be individually caught up during the first year.

english_requirements.notes: Bachelor's degree of a university programme taught in English or university entrance qualification received in an English programme or Bachelor's thesis written in English.

course_content_summary: The international Master's programme in Geophysics at KIT is research-oriented, has a strong focus on exploration and earthquake seismology as well as natural hazards and brings together theory and field experiments. Students studying in the international Master's programme in Geophysics benefit from the strong expertise in exploration and earthquake seismology, for which the Geophysical Institute of KIT has been known worldwide for more than 50 years. Compulsory courses cover a variety of aspects of earthquake seismology and seismics and can be combined with optional courses, field classes and summer schools or workshops to focus on individual interests. This provides our students with the opportunity to cover the full range of geophysics from data acquisition to modelling, inversion and interpretation. The programme is taught entirely in English.

program_name: International Master's Programme in Infrastructure Planning – MIP

english_requirements.notes: Other test formats than TOEFL or IELTS or confirmations from universities (English as the medium of instruction) are not accepted. Test must not be older than 24 months.

course_content_summary: Infrastructure facilities, transport, water, waste management, urban planning, regional planning, economic, social, ecological, management requirements, interdisciplinary approach, Integrated Planning, Transportation Planning and Modelling, Regional and Urban Planning, Economics, Statistics and Information Processing, GIS and Data Acquisition, Methodological Aspects of Planning, Tendering, Contracting and Project Management, Development Policy and Planning, Ecological Aspects of Infrastructure Planning, Energy Supply, Water and Waste Management, Project Appraisal, Planning and Financing, Integrated Case Study.

university_name: Hochschule für Technik Stuttgart - University of Applied Sciences

english_requirements.notes: Native English speakers exempt

course_content_summary: This Master's program in International Project Management (IPM) is designed for professionals in construction, infrastructure, or engineering, preparing them for leading roles in complex international projects. It attracts experienced postgraduates and offers two specializations: International Building Project Management (IBPM) for superstructure projects and International Infrastructure Technology & Management (IITM) for large-scale infrastructure projects. Students acquire soft skills, in-depth knowledge of tools, processes, and organizational methods essential for successful international project delivery. An optional additional semester in the UK can lead to an MBA from Edinburgh Napier University.

english_requirements.notes: For more information and other language tests that can be recognised, please refer to the application information website: www.uni-hohenheim.de/en/application-landecol-info.

course_content_summary: Landscape ecology, biodiversity, ecosystems, environmental change, climate, soils, land use, nature conservation, fieldwork, laboratory experiments, computer-based activities, project work, team-based work, research projects, environmental management, ecological problems, quantitative skills, sustainable use of natural resources, analytical problem-solving, research in ecology, biodiversity science, environmental protection, geographical analysis, landscape planning, research communication, environmental education.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Requires fundamental knowledge in both chemistry (physical, inorganic and organic chemistry) and biology (cell biology, genetics, biochemistry), both including laboratory courses. Good assessments are required in organic chemistry, biochemistry, and molecular biology.

english_requirements.notes: Applicants with a Bachelor's degree from Australia, the United Kingdom, Ireland, Canada, Malta, New Zealand, Singapore, South Africa or the United States do not have to prove their English language skills.

course_content_summary: Life Science is an interdisciplinary and research-oriented joint study program of chemistry and biology. It focuses on modern biomolecular research, building on fundamental knowledge in both fields. Key topics include organic chemistry, physical chemistry, inorganic chemistry, cell biology, genetics, biochemistry, molecular biology, bioinformatics, immunology, biochemical pharmacology, microbiology, cellular chemistry, proteomics, carbohydrate chemistry, protein chemistry, and nucleic acids chemistry. The program includes obligatory theory and laboratory courses.

english_requirements.notes: For the English Linguistics area of specialisation: English language skills level C1 (CEFR)

course_content_summary: Master's degree programme Linguistics, two-year, four semesters, 120 ECTS credits. Six specialisations, three in English: General Linguistics, English Linguistics, double-degree with Università di Verona (applied linguistics). Focus on general theories, linguistics research, universal structures of human speech, linguistic knowledge application to individual languages, language universals, comparative language observations.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: A Bachelor's degree of 210 ECTS is required. If the Bachelor's degree is 180 ECTS, an additional 30 ECTS can be obtained through a pre-semester course or a semester abroad.

english_requirements.notes: German Abitur accepted as proof.

german_requirements.notes: German skills are not required for the course, but they are highly recommended.

course_content_summary: The MA in Business Management program aims to elevate Bachelor's knowledge to a management level, optimizing career prospects. It is based on the system-oriented St. Gallen Management Approach, preparing students to successfully manage diverse companies and institutions. Specializations include International Sales Management, People and Culture, and IT Management. A four-semester version offers a specialization in Digital Leadership, with an option for a double degree in cooperation with Sapienza Università di Roma. The curriculum balances theory and practice, involving local businesses in design and teaching. Lectures utilize case studies, and students benefit from professors' and partner companies' hands-on management experience. The program includes a mandatory internship or research project, serving as preparation for the Master's thesis. A Bachelor's degree with at least 30 ECTS in business studies is required.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: equivalent in content and scope to those in the BSc course in Computer Science in Tübingen
equivalent in content and scope to those in the BSc course in Computer Science in Tübingen

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur" certificate with proof of six (G8) or seven years (G9) of English language instruction; Higher education entrance qualification obtained in Great Britain, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, New Zealand

course_content_summary: The Master’s programme in Machine Learning offers a wide choice of courses in machine learning and general computer science. Students attend lectures, seminars, and project lab courses, engaging in basic and applied research and current topics in machine learning. The curriculum leverages strong research presence in machine learning (Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems, Max Planck Institute for Biological Cybernetics, Excellence Cluster "Machine Learning: New Perspectives for Science'', Tübingen AI Center, ELLIS Institute, Cyber Valley, IMPRS for Intelligent Systems) and wider computer science. Training is based on recent insights and research questions. Project work and the Master’s thesis offer opportunities to develop code for research purposes and scientific projects. Interdisciplinarity is key, with Master’s theses supervised by professors from any computer science subfield.

english_requirements.notes: German "Abitur", including at least six (G8) or seven (G9) years of English or Bachelor's degree in English/American Studies or University degree obtained in the UK, Ireland, USA, Canada, Australia, or New Zealand

course_content_summary: Global management, strategic interaction, decision-making processes, adaptation strategies, management tools, game-theoretic concepts, strategic behaviour of competing firms, business strategies, managerial economics, personnel economics, industrial organisation, analytical skills, principal-agent theory, performance measures, incentive systems, human capital management.

english_requirements.notes: Successful completion of a study programme with English as language of instruction and examination of at least 40% of the courses and examinations or a University Entrance Qualification (HZB) from a school with English as the language of instruction serves as proof. Test results obtained no more than two years prior to the application deadline are accepted.

german_requirements.notes: Required if applying for the German-English track. No German language skills required for the English track.

course_content_summary: The Mannheim Master in Management (MMM) prepares students for management positions with flexibility and an international focus. Students can tailor their studies to professional goals, choosing courses from Accounting and Taxation, Finance, Banking and Insurance, Information Systems, Management, Marketing and Sales, and Operations Management. Core courses include Business Economics I and II, Decision Analysis, Corporate Social Responsibility, and Applied Econometrics or Empirical Methods. An elective module allows for reduced business administration courses. Most courses are taught in English, with options for an entirely English track, semester abroad, and eight double degree programs (Bocconi, LUISS, CBS, ESSEC, NCU, NHH, Queen's, USC).

program_name: Mannheim Master in Operations and Supply Chain Management

accepted_tests.test_name: Universität Mannheim Service und Marketing GmbH language test

english_requirements.notes: The successful completion of a study programme with English as language of instruction and examination of at least 40 % of the courses and examinations or a University Entrance Qualification (HZB) from a school with English as the language of instruction serves as proof. We accept only test results obtained no more than two years prior to the application deadline.

course_content_summary: The Mannheim Master in Operations and Supply Chain Management (MMOSCM) focuses on understanding and managing complex supply chains, covering procurement, production, logistics, and service. It combines analytical methods (descriptive, predictive, prescriptive) with global issues like sustainability and digital transformation. Graduates become experts in developing efficient and sustainable networks of companies and processes.

program_name: Mannheim Master's in Finance, Accounting and Taxation

accepted_tests.test_name: Universität Mannheim Service und Marketing GmbH language test

english_requirements.notes: Successful completion of a study programme with English as language of instruction and examination of at least 40% of the courses and examinations or a University Entrance Qualification (HZB) from a school with English as the language of instruction serve as proof. Test results accepted only if obtained no more than two years prior to the application deadline.

course_content_summary: Corporate finance, accounting, taxation, data analysis, empirical methods, strategic decisions, modern finance, tax issues, sustainable decision-making, well-founded decisions.

english_requirements.notes: English language university entrance exam (e.g., A-levels) or a degree (e.g. a Bachelor's degree) with at least 90 ECTS taught in English accepted as proof.

course_content_summary: Applied research in Political Science, empirical-analytical approach, quantitative methodology, political system of the EU, International Relations, International Political Economy, International Comparative Social Research, Comparative Political Behaviour, Political Sociology, Quantitative Methods. Specialisations in Comparative Politics (political institutions of European democracies, EU member states' legislation, electoral systems, political communication, participation, electoral studies, coalition theories in Europe) and International Politics (European integration, European governance, African politics, conflict studies, political economy).

english_requirements.notes: For applicants whose native language is NOT English, language certificates are required.

course_content_summary: Master of Arts in Jewellery focuses on traditional know-how, craftsmanship, state-of-the-art technology, pioneering research and development. Emphasizes experimentation, analysis, critical reflection, aesthetic self-confidence, independent thinking, and confident craftsmanship. The program expands the concept of 'jewellery' within contexts of material, technology, body, space, series, one-offs, tradition, and innovation. It is interdisciplinary, relating jewellery to social development, culture, and the environment. Open to jewellery and object makers with a BA degree or diploma, also welcoming applications from accessories, fashion, or product design with an affinity for jewellery.

program_name: Master of Arts in Marketing and Artificial Intelligence

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: At least 50% of the ECTS from the Bachelor’s degree (180 or 210 ECTS)

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers exempt

course_content_summary: English-taught Master's course, Marketing, Artificial Intelligence, generative AI, marketing automation, traditional marketing, strategic marketing, operational marketing, business cases, partner companies, International Marketing, Strategic Marketing, Consumer Behaviour, Brand Management, Marketing and Social Media Analytics, R, Python, Generative AI, Conversational AI Applications, Creativity, Entrepreneurship, Sustainability, Cross-Functional Skills, Self-Development, modern marketing, double degree, ESC Clermont-Ferrand, France.

specific_ects_requirements.domain_name: Quantitative Methods, Empirical Social Research, Statistics or Mathematics
specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: Quantitative theses are counted with a maximum of 6 ECTS credits towards the required 24 ECTS credits; crediting also requires a certificate from the thesis supervisor confirming the empirical-quantitative nature of the thesis.

accepted_tests.test_name: The European Language Certificate (telc) - English University
Universität Mannheim Service and Marketing GmbH language test

english_requirements.notes: Successful completion of a program of study with English as the language of instruction and examination or a University Entrance Qualification (HZB) from a school based in a school system in which the official language is English serve as proof. Continuous attendance of English classes throughout the German secondary school education with a minimum average of 11 points (proof must not be older than five years).

course_content_summary: The MA Sociology degree program offers a two-year research-oriented education focusing on empirical-analytical approaches, quantitative methods, and comparative research. The curriculum emphasizes training in empirical analysis grounded in theory and rigorous methods. Substantive areas include Family, Education & Work, Migration & Integration, Economy & Social Welfare State, and Social Inequality. The program is divided into seven modules: Sociological Research Fields, Foundations of Sociological Theory, Cross-Sectional Data Analysis, Longitudinal Data Analysis, Research Design, research project, and the Master's thesis. All classes are taught in English.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: to be demonstrated by corresponding modules or courses from the Bachelor's or equivalent degree programme or by the successfully completed pre-Master's course for English-taught Master's degree programmes at Ulm University or by equivalent competences

course_content_summary: Research-oriented Master's degree in Biology, primarily taught in English. Opportunity to determine focal point, individual tailoring through module combinations. Specialised approach in molecular biosciences or comprehensive approach combining different areas. Compulsory lectures.

specific_ects_requirements.condition_comment: at least 5 ECTS credits must be from physics

accepted_tests.test_name: Business English Certificate (BEC) Vantage Cambridge exam
English for Speakers of Other Languages (ESOL, Cambridge University)

english_requirements.notes: Native speakers of English are exempt.

course_content_summary: Master of Science in Biophysics, research-oriented, interdisciplinary, explores new areas between physics and biology, investigates complexity of life, analyzes with physical methods, understands molecular interactions and control mechanisms, learns about life processes, physical principles, and disease bases. Provides comprehensive education, hands-on research, prepares for interdisciplinary life sciences, trains in quantitative thinking and state-of-the-art experimental techniques. Deepens knowledge in biophysics, chemistry, biochemistry, supports independent research. Research environment based on collaborations between physics, chemistry, biology. Course subjects include cellular biophysics, bioinformatics, neurobiology, cellular and molecular biology, nanomaterials, polymer physics.
